questions.question: ‘Kargil Vijay Diwas’ is celebrated on which date in India
questions.explanation: India celebrates the ‘Kargil Vijay Diwas’ on July 26 every year, to celebrate the 23-year anniversary of its victory over Pakistan in the Kargil War. The Kargil War was fought against intruders from Pakistan, who had crossed the Line of Control (LoC) into Indian Territory in 1998. On July 26, 1999, the Pakistan forces were forced to withdraw from the Indian Territory.

questions.question: Which animal features in the 36th National Games 2022, to be held in Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has been approved to host the 36th National Games between September 27 and October 10, in 2022. The last edition of the National Games was organised in Kerala in 2015. Gir Lion and Statue of Unity with Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel are seen in the logo of the Games. IOA (Indian Olympic Association) accepted the proposal of Gujarat for hosting the event after 7 years.

questions.question: Kais Saied, who was seen in the news, is the President of which country?
questions.explanation: Tunisia passed a referendum on a new constitution that gives its President almost unlimited powers. Tunisia’s President Kais Saied celebrated the victory of the referendum after favourable exit poll. Tunisia, or the Republic of Tunisia, is the northern-most country in Africa and its capital is Tunis.

questions.question: Which country organises the annual military exercises named ‘Han Kuang’?
questions.explanation: Taiwan’s main annual ‘Han Kuang’ exercises commenced recently and Taiwan’s President Tsai Ing-wen oversaw the largest annual naval and air exercises. 20 naval vessels including frigates and destroyers fired shells to simulate intercepting and attacking an invading force. It was organised off Taiwan’s northeastern coast, amidst rising military pressure from China.

questions.question: Which country unveiled a rare and well preserved Roman-era coin dated ‘year eight’?
questions.explanation: Israeli archaeologists unveiled a rare and well preserved Roman-era coin dating back 1,850 years. It depicts the moon goddess Luna, the first such find in Israeli waters. The coin minted in Alexandria in present-day Egypt features the zodiac sign Cancer below Luna and the era’s Roman emperor, Antoninus Pius, on the other side. It is dated ‘year eight’ referring to the eighth year of Antoninus Pius’s rule, part of an era known as Pax Romana.

questions.question: ‘CRISM’ and ‘MRO’ which were seen in the news are associated with exploration of which celestial body?
questions.explanation: According to National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA), scientists released the first pieces of the multispectral maps made by the Compact Reconnaissance Imaging Spectrometer for Mars (CRISM),. NASA’s Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter (MRO) has been mapping minerals on the red planet Mars for the last 16 years with the help of CRISM.

questions.question: Which global association recognised the right to a clean, healthy and sustainable environment as a human right?
questions.explanation: The United Nations General Assembly adopted a historic resolution that recognised that everyone, everywhere, has a human right to live in a clean, healthy and sustainable environment. India has voted in favour of the UN General Assembly resolution but raised its concerns over the procedure and substance of the resolution.

questions.question: Birmingham, which hosts the 22nd Commonwealth Games, is a city of which country?
questions.explanation: The 22nd Commonwealth Games commenced in the city of Birmingham, United Kingdom. The Indian contingent, consisting of 215 athletes competing across 16 disciplines, was led by double Olympic medallist badminton star PV Sindhu and men’s hockey captain Manpreet Singh. The Prince of Wales declared the Games open. In the 2018 Games held in Australia, the Indian athletes recorded their second-best performance ever.

questions.question: Which company is set to establish first-ever India House at Paris Olympics 2024?
questions.explanation: Reliance Industries (RIL) and Indian Olympic Association (IOA) are set to together establish the first-ever India House at Paris Olympics 2024. RIL announced a long-term partnership with IOA to elevate the performances of Indian athletes, support national sports federations and build credentials of India as a global sporting nation. RIL will support Indian athletes in all major multi-sport events India is also be hosting the 140th IOC Session in June 2023 at the Jio World Centre in Mumbai.

questions.question: Sebastian Vettel, who announced retirement recently, is a racing driver from which country?
questions.explanation: Four-time world champion Sebastian Vettel has recently announced his retirement from Formula One at the end of the year. The 35 year old driver competes in Formula One for Aston Martin, at present. During his time at Red Bull, he won four consecutive titles from 2010 to 2013.
